method_atom: A technique for decomposing the representations of a large symmetry structure into those of an embedded sub-structure and computing the resulting decomposition multiplicities in closed form: the smaller structure is enlarged to an infinite-dimensional extension of itself, from which a reflection-group alternating-sum algorithm (analogous to the one used for combining two representations into a product) is derived, together with integral/contour representations of the multiplicities and a diagonalized-counting formula that relates them to representations of an associative fusion algebra.
object_atom: Finite-dimensional semi-simple Lie algebras and the embeddings of one such algebra into another, and, in the physics-facing part, the chiral fusion rings of Wess-Zumino-Witten conformal field theory models at level k together with their non-negative-integer-matrix representations.
brief_reason: The embed-extend-and-count machinery for decomposition multiplicities (infinite-extension trick, reflection-group alternating sum, and Verlinde-type diagonalized counting via a fusion algebra) is the transferable method, while the semi-simple Lie algebras and WZW fusion rings are the domain-specific objects; the separation is partly by abstraction since branching via affine extension is intrinsically an algebraic construction.
